_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion. It does not mean the criterion has been verified by a formal audit. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status.

criteria.title: Non-text Content
criteria.evidence: The .rating root carries role='img' and aria-label with a human-readable rating value (e.g. '3.5 out of 5 stars'). All .rating__star elements and SVGs carry aria-hidden='true' to suppress redundant announcements. Enforced by RATING-A11Y-001 and RATING-A11Y-002.

criteria.title: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.evidence: Stars are non-text content — WCAG 1.4.11 applies. See 1.4.11 entry. Disabled state (40% opacity) is exempt per WCAG 1.4.3 inactive component exception.

criteria.title: Non-text Contrast
criteria.evidence: Default gold (#DBA102) on white (#FFF) = ~2.0:1 contrast. This is below the required 3:1 threshold for non-text UI components. Brand green (#1B8354) on white = ~4.58:1 — passes. Empty star (#E5E7EB) represents absent content, not a UI boundary — may be exempt. RISK: Default (gold) variant does not meet 1.4.11.

criteria.title: Keyboard
criteria.evidence: Display rating: not keyboard interactive — no keyboard requirement. Interactive rating (see contract §10): uses native radio inputs which are keyboard operable by default. RATING-STRUCT-001 warns if non-semantic elements are used for interactive mode.

criteria.title: Focus Visible
criteria.evidence: No focus state is defined in the manifest. For display-only usage, no focus indicator is needed (component is not interactive). For interactive usage, focus styles must be added. RATING-A11Y-003 flags suppressed focus on interactive rating. Focus state not in manifest — see analysis.md Known Issues.

criteria.title: Name, Role, Value
criteria.evidence: Display: role='img' on root + aria-label provides name and role. Value embedded in aria-label string. Interactive: native radio inputs provide name/role/value natively. Enforced by RATING-A11Y-001.

standards.name: Platform Design System Internal Standards
standards._note: Internal platform rules. IDs are component-local. See standards/platform-code.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Component must use design tokens for all visual values
criteria.evidence: All color, spacing, and size values in rating.css reference --rating-* CSS custom properties with fallback values from the manifest. Hardcoded value detection enforced by RATING-TOKEN-001. Note: --rating-* tokens are proposed and must be added to token.css.

criteria.title: RTL layout support
criteria.evidence: Container uses gap (direction-agnostic). Half-star clip direction pending RTL design confirmation. [dir='rtl'] override added in rating.css with a TODO. Physical directional properties are not used. Enforced by RATING-RTL-001.

criteria.title: Semantic HTML element for interactive usage
criteria.evidence: Display rating uses div with role='img'. Interactive rating must use fieldset + radio inputs per contract §10. RATING-STRUCT-001 warns on non-semantic interactive usage.

criteria.title: Minimum touch target 24×24px
criteria.evidence: Small size (--sm) star token is 24px. Medium is 32px. Large is 48px. All meet the 24×24px minimum touch target. No automated rule implemented yet.

standards.name: Platform Accessibility Internal Standards
standards._note: Accessibility-specific internal standards. See standards/accessibility.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Arabic language support
criteria.evidence: Rating component contains no text by default. The aria-label value is provided by the consuming application and must be in the appropriate language. Documented in contract §14.

criteria.title: Screen reader announcement must be meaningful
criteria.evidence: role='img' with aria-label on root provides a single meaningful announcement. Individual stars are aria-hidden. Screen reader does not iterate over 5 stars individually.

variant_compliance_notes.default (gold): WCAG 1.4.11: #DBA102 on white = ~2.0:1, below the 3:1 non-text contrast threshold. Must be reviewed with design team before production deployment.
variant_compliance_notes.empty stars: #E5E7EB represents absent rating content (no stars). Exempt from 1.4.11 as it is not a UI control boundary — it conveys absence of rating rather than a meaningful UI boundary.
variant_compliance_notes.half stars: Half fill uses clip-path CSS technique. Visual output matches Figma design. Contrast of colored half applies same as filled variant.
variant_compliance_notes.disabled (all variants): WCAG 1.4.3 and 1.4.11 exempt inactive UI components. Low contrast of disabled state is intentional.
